title: ～上に
titleEn: Express in addition to, besides, not only... but also
structure: Verb-plain + 上に, い-Adjective + 上に, な-Adjective + な上に, Noun + の上に
structureEn: Verb-plain + 上に, い-Adjective + 上に, な-Adjective + な上に, Noun + の上に
explanation: Dùng để bổ sung một đặc điểm hoặc tình huống nữa vào điều đã nêu, thường cùng hướng tốt hoặc xấu. Có thể hiểu là “đã... lại còn...”, “hơn nữa”. Cấu trúc: Verb-plain + 上に, い-Adjective + 上に, な-Adjective + な上に, Noun + の上に.
explanationEn: The ～上に grammar point is used to indicate that there is an additional piece of information or situation besides the first one. It can be translated as in addition to, besides, or not only... but also. It can be used with verbs, い-adjectives, な-adjectives, and nouns
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～上は
titleEn: Expresses now that, since, or once
structure: Verb-casual + 上は, い-Adjective + 上は, な-Adjective + な上は, Noun + の上は
structureEn: Verb-casual + 上は, い-Adjective + 上は, な-Adjective + な上は, Noun + の上は
explanation: Dùng khi đã rơi vào điều kiện/tình huống A thì phải làm hoặc chấp nhận B. Sắc thái quyết tâm/trách nhiệm: “một khi đã... thì...”. Cấu trúc: Verb-casual + 上は, い-Adjective + 上は, な-Adjective + な上は, Noun + の上は.
explanationEn: The ～上は grammar point is used to express that since a certain condition or situation exists, a certain action or result follows. It can be translated as now that, since, or once in English. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～以上
titleEn: Expresses since or as long as in a conditional context
structure: Verb-casual + 以上, い-Adjective + 以上, な-Adjective + な以上, Noun + の以上
structureEn: Verb-casual + 以上, い-Adjective + 以上, な-Adjective + な以上, Noun + の以上
explanation: Dùng khi một điều kiện đã đúng thì kết quả, trách nhiệm hoặc hành động phía sau là điều tất yếu. Có thể hiểu là “một khi đã...”, “vì đã...”. Cấu trúc: Verb-casual + 以上, い-Adjective + 以上, な-Adjective + な以上, Noun + の以上.
explanationEn: The ～以上 grammar point is used to express a condition or requirement, and it can be translated as since, as long as, or if. It often emphasizes that if a certain condition is met, a certain action or consequence will follow
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～以来
titleEn: Indicates something happening since, ever since, or after a particular time
structure: Verb-past + 以来, い-Adjective + 以来, Noun + 以来
structureEn: Verb-past + 以来, い-Adjective + 以来, Noun + 以来
explanation: Dùng để nói một trạng thái hoặc hành động tiếp diễn liên tục từ một mốc trong quá khứ đến hiện tại. Có thể hiểu là “kể từ khi...”. Cấu trúc: Verb-past + 以来, い-Adjective + 以来, Noun + 以来.
explanationEn: The ～以来 grammar point is used to express a period of time that has continued uninterrupted since a certain point in the past. It can be translated as since, ever since, or after. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, an adjective, or a noun
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～切る
titleEn: To do something completely or thoroughly
explanation: Gắn sau thân động từ để nói làm xong hoàn toàn, dùng hết hoặc vượt qua đến cùng. Sắc thái hoàn tất triệt để. Cấu trúc: Verb-stem + 切る.
explanationEn: The ～切る grammar point is added to the verb stem (masu-form) to express doing something completely or thoroughly, often implying a strong sense of finishing or using up everything
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～反面
titleEn: Express contrast between two aspects; on the one hand, on the other hand
structure: Verb-casual + 反面, い-Adjective + 反面, な-Adjective + である反面, Noun + である反面
structureEn: Verb-casual + 反面, い-Adjective + 反面, な-Adjective + である反面, Noun + である反面
explanation: Dùng để nêu hai mặt trái ngược của cùng một sự vật hoặc tình huống. Có thể hiểu là “mặt khác”, “trái lại thì”. Cấu trúc: Verb-casual + 反面, い-Adjective + 反面, な-Adjective + である反面, Noun + である反面.
explanationEn: The ～反面 grammar point is used to show a contrast between two aspects of something. It can be translated as on the one hand or on the other hand
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～向け
explanation: Dùng để chỉ đối tượng mục tiêu mà sản phẩm, dịch vụ, tài liệu hoặc hoạt động hướng tới. Có thể hiểu là “dành cho”, “hướng tới”. Cấu trúc: Noun + 向け.
explanationEn: The ～向け grammar point is used to indicate the target or intended audience for a product, service, or event. It can be translated as for, aimed at, or intended for
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～恐れがある
titleEn: Express a concern or fear that something undesirable might occur
explanation: Dùng để nói có nguy cơ hoặc khả năng xảy ra điều không mong muốn. Sắc thái trang trọng, hay gặp trong cảnh báo, tin tức, văn bản. Cấu trúc: Verb-dictionary form + 恐れがある.
explanationEn: The ～恐れがある grammar point indicates that there is a concern or fear that something undesirable or negative might happen. It is often used for potential problems or risks, and can be translated as there is a risk or there is a possibility
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)
